heroHeadline: Trump Cancels Pakistan Talks, Leaving Araghchi Stranded in Islamabad — 'We Have All the Cards'
heroSubtitle: On April 25, President Trump abruptly canceled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's planned trip to Islamabad for a second round of indirect US-Iran nuclear negotiations, leaving Iranian FM Araghchi — who had already arrived in Pakistan anticipating the talks — without a counterpart. Trump declared 'We have all the cards,' signaling a sharp hardening of the US negotiating posture. The collapse of the diplomatic track raises the prospect of Iran accelerating its nuclear program as leverage, while no new date for resumed talks has been set. The move came as the region remained on edge with three US carriers in theater and the dual-blockade standoff unresolved.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-25. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, NBC News, Euronews, UN News, Axios, Washington Post, Army Recognition, France 24, SOF News, Pakistan Today, Iran Wire, Voice of Emirates, The New York Times, The Guardian, The Japan Times.
